title: Preparation and transformation of chemically competent Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ea2rn","text":"Calcium chloride (CaCl2) transformation is a laboratory technique in prokaryotic (bacterial) cell biology. The addition of calcium chloride to a cell suspension promotes the binding of plasmid DNA to lipopolysaccharides (LPS).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":22,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a sterile flask, add 200 mL distilled water
Quickly thaw a single reaction tube with 100 µL hyper-competent cells in Inoue-DMSO
Measure 25 mL ( 1 Molarity (M) ) Calcium chloride Materials: Calcium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C3881
Filter sterilize solution with a filter (0.2 µm) and store refrigerated ( 4 °C )
Fill flask with distilled water to 250 mL
Add measured reagents and mix for 00:05:00

Prepare a culture of E. coli on an LB agar plate. Pick a single colony, and inoculate in 500 mL LB broth in a 1000 mL flask. Incubate at 18 °C with shaking at 100 rpm overnight, until OD 600 reaches 0.6
Incubate cells on ice for 00:10:00
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 16 mL Calcium chloride transformation buffer
Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Place tubes on ice for 00:20:00
Aliquot entire culture volume into 50 mL canonical tubes
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 10 mL DMSO-Inoue per tube

Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Meanwhile, prepare 50 mL glycerol-Calcium chloride transformation buffer by diluting 7.5 mL Glycerol in 42.5 mL Calcium chloride transformation buffer Materials: Glycerol MP Biomedicals Catalog #194680 (CAS: 56-81-5)
Centrifuge tubes with 5000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Discard supernatant and resuspend with 8 mL Inoue transformation buffer. Pool into two tubes
Snap freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen using a floating foam tube rack (Southern labware #HS2166)
Aliquot 100 µL cell suspension into sterile 500 µL screw cap reaction tubes (Sarstedt #72.704.200). While creating aliquots, keep original tubes, and aliquots on ice, until snap-freeze take place
Incubate cells on ice for 00:30:00
Transfer aliquots storage box, and place in an ultra-low temperature freezer or vapor-phase nitrogen tank Store tubes in 50 mL canonical tubes, or similar containers
Incubate cells on ice for 00:30:00
Mix 1-5 µL plasmid (ligation product) Do not exceed 5% of the volume competent cells Use up-to 25 ng per 50 µL of competent cells
Heat-shock cells at 42 °C for 00:00:30 , followed by 00:02:00 at 4 °C immediatly
Add 500 µL prewarmed S. O. C. medium and incubate for 37 °C at 200 rpm for 01:00:00
Add desired amount of suspension on LB plates with ampicillin (100 µg/mL) and incubate overnight
creator: Andreas Sagen
